title: Union
summary: Union is an interoperability protocol focused on trust-minimized cross-chain communication. Arcium includes it in ecosystem coverage, but the public Union materials I reviewed do not document a specific Arcium-powered module.
description:
Union's official documentation frames the project around interoperability and trust-minimized cross-chain communication. While the public landing surface is lighter than some peers, the docs are enough to establish Union as a serious infrastructure project in the interop layer.

Arcium includes Union in the encrypted interoperability ecosystem. That is enough for Atlas inclusion, but not enough to claim a project-side Arcium integration from current public materials.

bodySections.title: What_Atlas_Reviewed
bodySections.body: Union's public-facing information is more documentation-heavy than marketing-heavy. That is often a sign of infra maturity, but it also means Atlas should stay disciplined and avoid making claims not explicitly documented.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium lists Union among interoperability ecosystem partners. That supports inclusion in Atlas, but without a project-side implementation note the safest classification remains ecosystem project.

statusNote: Arcium includes Union in its interoperability ecosystem coverage, but I did not find a current Union page that documents an explicit Arcium-powered product surface.

sources.label: Union Website
sources.note: Official site for Union's interoperability protocol.

sources.label: Arcium Encrypted Ecosystem
sources.note: Arcium lists Union among interoperability ecosystem partners.
